title: Aberdeen AAS Sales Tools, Proposals, and Pricing Economics (August 2001): Repricing Memo, Client Profile Template, Impact Proposal, Market Knowledge Proposal Template, and Consulting Revenue Analysis
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
subject_domain: pricing strategy; sales templates; consulting economics; client templates
abstract: Five internal Aberdeen Group sales-and-pricing artifacts from August 2001, anchored by Kastner's August 11, 2001 repricing memo establishing the simplified Master Price List with five retainer types and a new $6,000 six-month introductory AAS. A consulting-revenue-analysis spreadsheet provides rare primary-source unit-economics data: headcount, per-diem cost basis, add-on day rates, and contribution margins by labor class, rolling up to $1,112,500 GMR and $778,095 contribution at 5 consulting days per quarter. Supporting templates include the Client Profile (organizational structure, competitive positioning, financial highlights), the Aberdeen Impact 2-page deliverable (one-year web license, single review cycle), and the Market Knowledge Proposal Template (1-day on-site agenda, 4-6 page report, optional Strategy Validation project).
importance_rationale: Direct primary-source pricing and unit-economics data — rare survival of a tech analyst firm's actual contribution-margin model and per-diem cost basis from this era.
relevance_rationale: Ties the previously-archived AAS launch (batch 4) to the actual sales templates and unit economics that supported it; the consulting-revenue-analysis is a uniquely valuable financial primary source.
prescience_rationale: The contribution-margin-driven pricing tier model and the explicit Days 3-N billability optimization anticipated the unit-economics rigor that became standard in modern professional services pricing; the one-time introductory model is the precursor to the freemium-with-paid-conversion pattern dominant today.
